title: moringaceae
intro: The Moringaceae rank is a botanical family of flowering plants, commonly known as horseradish or ben oil trees. This family consists of about 13 to 15 species, primarily native to the Old World tropics and subtropics. Moringaceae plants are known for their unique characteristics, such as their ability to fix nitrogen and their use in traditional medicine. They are also a valuable source of oil and other edible products, making them an important crop in various regions around the world.
